content: Pneumonia is classified by acquisition setting: Community-acquired pneumonia (CAP) occurs outside hospitals or within 48 hours of admission; Hospital-acquired pneumonia (HAP) develops ≥48 hours after admission; Ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) occurs >48-72 hours after endotracheal intubation. CAP pathogens include Streptococcus pneumoniae (most common), Haemophilus influenzae, atypical organisms (Mycoplasma pneumoniae, Legionella, Chlamydophila), and respiratory viruses. HAP/VAP pathogens include Pseudomonas aeruginosa, MRSA, Enterobacteriaceae, and Acinetobacter. Aspiration pneumonia involves anaerobes from oropharyngeal flora.
